steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence does two things: it raises a possible interpretation (‘fast painting accounts for the Highwaymen's ethereal qualities’) and immediately rejects it (‘tempting but inaccurate’). The correct answer must capture both moves — raising and then refuting an interpretation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It details evidence that contradicts a claim mentioned earlier in the text about a long-standing disagreement within a group of artists.: The text contains no mention of a disagreement among the Highwaymen; this fabricates a conflict not present in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — It explains how an artist mentioned earlier in the text developed a distinctive style by adapting a particular approach to painting originated by his colleagues.: The underlined portion is about the effect of the technique on the group's aesthetic, not about how any individual developed his style. It also reverses the direction — Hair originated fast painting, not his colleagues. ✗
  • Choice C — It considers and rebuts an interpretation of the effect of a painting technique mentioned earlier in the text on the perception of work by a group of artists.: This matches precisely: the sentence entertains the interpretation that fast painting caused the ethereal quality, then disproves it with the Roy McLendon counterexample. ✓
  • Choice D — It establishes a contrast between the aesthetic qualities of works by artists who were central to a movement introduced earlier in the text and those of an artist who was more peripheral to that movement.: The text never distinguishes between central and peripheral Highwaymen; McLendon is presented simply as a member of the group, not as marginal to it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
